---
title: "Liam Kirkpatrick"
description-meta: "Liam Kirkpatrick's Personal Website"
image: "files/images/basic/headshot1.jpg"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 85%
  links:
    - icon: globe
      text: Uprofile
      href: https://ess.uw.edu/people/liam-kirkpatrick/
      target: _blank
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:liamkp@uw.com    
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/liam-r-kirkpatrick/
      target: _blank
    - icon: instagram
      text: Instagram
      href: https://www.instagram.com/kirkpatrick.liam/
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"   # use academic icons, "" are needed, you also need to install the fontawesome extension
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6476-5331
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ldlw-aEAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
      target: _blank
#    - text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} Research Gate"
#      href: https://www.researchgate.net
#      target: _blank

listing:   # Comment the listing section and delete corresponding lines in the index.qmd file to remove the recent posts
  - id: recent-posts
    contents: 
      - posts
      - posts/post-links.yml
    exclude:
      title: ""
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
#    image-height: 150px
    page-size: 3 #set same as max-times to remove the page number setting
    fields: [image, date, title, subtitle]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

:::{#hero-heading}

Welcome to my personal website.

I’m Liam Kirkpatrick, a climate and glaciology PhD candidate currently studying in the Department of Earth and Space Sciences at the University of Washington. My interests lie at the intersection of climate science, geophysics, data science, and engineering. Broadly, my research goal is to improve and extend ice core records of past climate to improve our understanding of anthropogenic climate change.

I’ve made this site to share a bit about myself and my research, everything from fieldwork to lab work, fun youtube videos to nerdy science explainers.

I hope you enjoy, and please reach out with questions!

:::

<!-- Delete the following section and delete corresponding lines in the yaml part to remove the recent posts -->
## Recent Posts
Check out my latest [First-Author Publications](/posts.html#category=publication), [Fieldwork Stories](/posts.html#category=fieldwork), [Media](/posts.html#category=media), and [More »](/posts.html)

:::{#recent-posts}
:::

<p align="right">[All Posts »](/posts.html)</p>
